steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both sound-exposure conditions (ship sounds alone and ship sounds + sonic pulses) caused similarly significant decreases in narwhal deep dives — the two conditions produced nearly identical results. The blank must explain why adding sonic pulses on top of ship sounds had little additional effect on narwhal behavior.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘sonic pulses can be heard at significantly greater ocean depths than ship sounds can’: If sonic pulses penetrate deeper, they would be expected to cause a greater additional disruption than ship sounds alone — that would predict a difference between the two conditions, not explain why they produced similar results. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘ship sounds contribute so much to the overall sound level that the addition of sonic pulses has little effect on the narwhals' auditory environment’: If ship sounds already dominate the acoustic environment, adding sonic pulses barely changes what the narwhals hear — which directly explains why the two conditions had nearly the same effect on diving. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘narwhals forage at shallower depths in the presence of ship sounds alone than in the presence of ship sounds coupled with sonic pulses’: This claims there is a difference between the two conditions, directly contradicting the "negligible difference" the passage describes. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘the narwhals weren't as sensitive to human-caused sounds as the researchers had predicted’: Both exposure conditions did significantly reduce deep dives, so narwhals clearly were sensitive to human-caused sounds. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
